proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_009==1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `feature_009==0`.
Cell lines with `feature_022==1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `feature_022==0`.
Cell lines with `feature_024==1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `feature_024==0`.
Cell lines with `feature_014==1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `feature_014==0`.
Cell lines with `feature_027==1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `feature_027==0`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS controlling for `feature_011`, `feature_010`, `feature_004`, `feature_005`, `feature_001`, `feature_016`, and `feature_026` gave coefficient -0.55456 for `feature_009`; unadjusted means were -0.805 for `feature_009==1` vs -0.250 for `feature_009==0`.
Adjusted OLS gave coefficient -0.348921 for `feature_022`; unadjusted means were -0.601 for `feature_022==1` vs -0.252 for `feature_022==0`.
Adjusted OLS gave coefficient -0.432598 for `feature_024`; unadjusted means were -0.686 for `feature_024==1` vs -0.253 for `feature_024==0`.
Adjusted OLS gave coefficient -0.309577 for `feature_014`; unadjusted means were -0.559 for `feature_014==1` vs -0.252 for `feature_014==0`.
Adjusted OLS gave coefficient -0.095473 for `feature_027`; unadjusted means were -0.353 for `feature_027==1` vs -0.257 for `feature_027==0`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The subgroup with `feature_009==1` and `feature_011==skin` has more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than all other cell lines.
The subgroup with `feature_022==1` and `feature_011==brain` has more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than all other cell lines.
The subgroup with `feature_024==1` and `feature_010==her2_enriched` has more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than all other cell lines.
The subgroups with `feature_014==1` and either `feature_011==pancreatic` or `feature_011==colorectal` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than all other cell lines.
The subgroup with `feature_027==1` and `feature_011==brain` has more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_009==1 & feature_011==skin` had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.8114 vs -0.2909 in all other lines (n=1966).
`feature_022==1 & feature_011==brain` had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.6117 vs -0.2740 in all other lines (n=815).
`feature_024==1 & feature_010==her2_enriched` had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.7525 vs -0.2944 in all other lines (n=142).
`feature_014==1 & feature_011==pancreatic` had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.5591 vs -0.3103 in all other lines (n=2913).
`feature_014==1 & feature_011==colorectal` had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.5493 vs -0.3136 in all other lines (n=2364).
`feature_027==1 & feature_011==brain` had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.4047 vs -0.2650 in all other lines (n=329).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_011==colorectal` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_035==1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `feature_035==0`.
Cell lines with high `feature_028` (`feature_028>=0.819`, the sample upper quartile) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than other cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_020==1` have less negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `feature_020==0`, suggesting suppression of KIF18A dependency.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_011==colorectal` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.7204 vs -0.2524 in non-colorectal lines (n=5816).
`feature_035==1` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.5447 vs -0.2526 for `feature_035==0` (n=9285).
High `feature_028` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.4694 vs -0.2526 in the remaining lines (n=12508).
`feature_020==1` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.2491 vs -0.3158 for `feature_020==0`; the positive effect means less dependency in `feature_020==1` lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_011==colorectal` and `feature_035==1` interact negatively for `dependency_KIF18A`, making the double-positive subgroup more dependent than expected from adding their separate effects.

`feature_011==colorectal` and high `feature_028` (`feature_028>=0.819`) interact negatively for `dependency_KIF18A`, making the joint subgroup more dependent than expected from adding their separate effects.
Within `feature_011==colorectal` cell lines, `feature_020==1` suppresses `dependency_KIF18A`, producing less negative scores than expected from the colorectal main effect.

analyses.result_summary: Two-predicate OLS interaction screen gave interaction coefficient -0.6822 for `feature_011==colorectal` by `feature_035==1`; the double-positive mean was -0.9425, while the other three cells were near -0.259, -0.253, and -0.252.
Two-predicate OLS interaction screen gave interaction coefficient -0.6691 for `feature_011==colorectal` by high `feature_028`; the joint subgroup mean was -0.9288.

Two-predicate OLS interaction screen gave interaction coefficient +0.5714 for `feature_011==colorectal` by `feature_020==1`; colorectal/`feature_020==1` mean was -0.2501 compared with -0.8255 for colorectal/`feature_020==0`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete high-dependency subgroup for `dependency_KIF18A` is `feature_011==colorectal`, `feature_035==1`, high `feature_028` (`feature_028>=0.819`), and `feature_020==0`; this subgroup has much more negative scores than all other cell lines.

The otherwise enabling subgroup `feature_011==colorectal`, `feature_035==1`, high `feature_028` (`feature_028>=0.819`) is not KIF18A-dependent when `feature_020==1`; its `dependency_KIF18A` scores are not more negative than the rest of the screen.

analyses.result_summary: The four-predicate subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.2990 vs -0.2530 in all other lines (n=2571).

Adjusted OLS including the four-predicate subgroup indicator plus main terms for colorectal status, `feature_035`, `feature_028`, `feature_020`, `feature_017`, `feature_016`, `feature_026`, subtype, culture, batch, and `feature_001` gave subgroup coefficient -1.03094.

The same enabling predicates with `feature_020==1` had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.2557 vs -0.3074 in the rest of the screen (n=562); the positive contrast refutes a dependency in this suppressed subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: One standard deviation higher `feature_016` is associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores after adjustment for lineage, subtype, culture, batch, `feature_001`, `feature_026`, and `feature_027`.

One standard deviation higher `feature_016` is associated with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores after adjustment for lineage, subtype, culture, batch, `feature_001`, `feature_026`, and `feature_003`.

One standard deviation higher `feature_023` is associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores after adjustment for `feature_018`, `feature_016`, `feature_026`, lineage, subtype, culture, batch, and `feature_001`.

One standard deviation higher `feature_003` is associated with more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores after adjustment for `feature_018`, `feature_023`, `feature_016`, `feature_026`, lineage, subtype, culture, batch, and `feature_001`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS for `dependency_TMED10` gave coefficient -0.02028 per SD of `feature_016`; model R^2 was only 0.0052, so the effect is statistically strong but small.
Adjusted OLS for `dependency_POLR2A` gave coefficient -0.06121 per SD of `feature_016`; model R^2 was 0.0313.
Adjusted OLS for `dependency_DHX9` gave coefficient -0.01703 per SD of `feature_023`.
Adjusted OLS for `dependency_RPL11` gave coefficient -0.05587 per SD of `feature_003`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unadjusted association in which `feature_018==1` has more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores remains negative after adjustment for `feature_023`, `feature_016`, `feature_026`, lineage, subtype, culture, batch, and `feature_001`.

The unadjusted association in which `feature_018==1` has more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores remains negative after adjustment for `feature_003`, `feature_023`, `feature_016`, `feature_026`, lineage, subtype, culture, batch, and `feature_001`.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted, `feature_018==1` had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.3491 vs -0.3307 (difference -0.0184, p=1.69e-05), but after adjustment the coefficient became +0.00291 with p=0.521.
Unadjusted, `feature_018==1` had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.3614 vs -0.2523 (difference -0.1091, p=5.71e-140), but after adjustment the coefficient became +0.00920 with p=0.168.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_BRAF`, the subgroup `feature_011==skin` and `feature_018==1` has a more negative interaction effect than expected from adding the two main effects.
For `dependency_EGFR`, the subgroup `feature_010==adenocarcinoma` and high `feature_019` (`feature_019>=0.478`) has a more negative interaction effect than expected from adding the two main effects.
For `dependency_ERBB2`, the subgroup `feature_011==colorectal` and high `feature_015` (`feature_015>=8.33`) has a more negative interaction effect than expected from adding the two main effects.
For `dependency_KRAS`, the subgroup `feature_010==not_applicable` and high `feature_034` (`feature_034>=4.13`) has a more negative interaction effect than expected from adding the two main effects.
For `dependency_RIT1`, the subgroup `feature_011==brain` and `feature_027==1` has a more negative interaction effect than expected from adding the two main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Systematic two-predicate heterogeneity screen over 73 predicates found this as a negative BRAF interaction: coefficient -0.05224, raw p=0.001031, Bonferroni-adjusted p>1 across 2452 tested pairs.
Systematic two-predicate screen found interaction coefficient -0.03961 for `feature_010==adenocarcinoma` by high `feature_019`; raw p=0.000789, Bonferroni-adjusted p>1.
Systematic two-predicate screen found interaction coefficient -0.04064 for `feature_011==colorectal` by high `feature_015`; raw p=0.000589, Bonferroni-adjusted p>1.
Systematic two-predicate screen found interaction coefficient -0.03212 for `feature_010==not_applicable` by high `feature_034`; raw p=0.0001457, Bonferroni-adjusted p=0.357.
Systematic two-predicate screen found interaction coefficient -0.05823 for `feature_011==brain` by `feature_027==1`; raw p=0.005638, Bonferroni-adjusted p>1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_KIF18A`, the systematic heterogeneity search will identify `feature_011==colorectal` and `feature_035==1` as a strongly negative pairwise interaction.
For `dependency_TMED10`, the subgroup `feature_010==her2_enriched` and `feature_014==1` changes dependency relative to the two main effects.
For `dependency_DHX9`, the subgroup `feature_014==1` and low `feature_026` (`feature_026<=0.919`) has a more negative interaction effect than expected from adding the two main effects.

For `dependency_POLR2A`, the subgroup with low `feature_026` (`feature_026<=0.919`) and low `feature_023` (`feature_023<=4.23`) has a less negative interaction effect than expected from adding the two main effects.
For `dependency_RPL11`, the subgroup `feature_010==squamous` and `feature_024==1` has a more negative interaction effect than expected from adding the two main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Systematic two-predicate screen found the strongest KIF18A interactions, including `feature_011==colorectal` by `feature_035==1` with coefficient -0.6822 and Bonferroni-adjusted p approximately 0.

Systematic two-predicate screen found a positive TMED10 interaction for `feature_010==her2_enriched` by `feature_014==1`: coefficient +0.1187, raw p=0.0002767, Bonferroni-adjusted p=0.679. The direction is less negative, not a dependency-concentrating subgroup.
Systematic two-predicate screen found DHX9 interaction coefficient -0.03003 for `feature_014==1` by low `feature_026`; raw p=0.0003851, Bonferroni-adjusted p=0.944.

Systematic two-predicate screen found POLR2A interaction coefficient +0.03800 for low `feature_026` by low `feature_023`; raw p=6.668e-06 and Bonferroni-adjusted p=0.0164. The robust effect is buffering/less negative, not stronger dependency.
Systematic two-predicate screen found RPL11 interaction coefficient -0.0990 for `feature_010==squamous` by `feature_024==1`; raw p=0.00037, Bonferroni-adjusted p=0.907.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The final supported subgroup for `dependency_BRAF` is all cell lines with `feature_009==1`, with no robust additional two-feature dependency-concentrating interaction.
The final supported subgroup for `dependency_EGFR` is all cell lines with `feature_022==1`, with no robust additional two-feature dependency-concentrating interaction.
The final supported subgroup for `dependency_ERBB2` is all cell lines with `feature_024==1`, with no robust additional two-feature dependency-concentrating interaction.
The final supported subgroup for `dependency_KRAS` is all cell lines with `feature_014==1`, with no robust additional two-feature dependency-concentrating interaction.
The final supported subgroup for `dependency_RIT1` is all cell lines with `feature_027==1`, with no robust additional two-feature dependency-concentrating interaction.
The final supported subgroup for `dependency_KIF18A` is `feature_011==colorectal`, `feature_035==1`, high `feature_028` (`feature_028>=0.819`), and `feature_020==0`.
For `dependency_TMED10`, no feature-defined dependency subgroup has a large or robust negative effect; only small common covariate shifts from `feature_016` and `feature_026` are supported.
For `dependency_DHX9`, the best-supported relationship is a modest linear association with higher `feature_023`, not a robust categorical dependency subgroup.
For `dependency_POLR2A`, the best-supported relationship is a stronger negative association with higher `feature_016`, plus one small buffering low-`feature_026`/low-`feature_023` interaction.
For `dependency_RPL11`, the best-supported relationship is a stronger negative association with higher `feature_003`, not a robust categorical dependency subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Final synthesis: adjusted `feature_009` coefficient for BRAF was -0.55456 (p approximately 0), while the best negative BRAF pairwise interaction failed Bonferroni correction.
Final synthesis: adjusted `feature_022` coefficient for EGFR was -0.34892 (p approximately 0), while the best negative EGFR pairwise interaction failed Bonferroni correction.
Final synthesis: adjusted `feature_024` coefficient for ERBB2 was -0.43260 (p approximately 0), while the best negative ERBB2 pairwise interaction failed Bonferroni correction.
Final synthesis: adjusted `feature_014` coefficient for KRAS was -0.30958 (p approximately 0), while the best negative KRAS pairwise interaction failed Bonferroni correction.
Final synthesis: adjusted `feature_027` coefficient for RIT1 was -0.09547 (p=6.03e-69), while the best negative RIT1 pairwise interaction failed Bonferroni correction.
Final synthesis: the four-predicate KIF18A subgroup had mean -1.2990 vs -0.2530 in the rest of the screen and adjusted coefficient -1.03094.
Final synthesis: TMED10 showed small adjusted effects for `feature_016` (-0.02028 per SD) and `feature_026` (-0.01240 per SD), model R^2=0.0052, and no Bonferroni-robust negative pairwise subgroup.
Final synthesis: DHX9 showed adjusted coefficient -0.01703 per SD of `feature_023`, while candidate pairwise dependency interactions did not survive correction.

Final synthesis: POLR2A showed adjusted coefficient -0.06121 per SD of `feature_016`; the only Bonferroni-robust pairwise interaction was positive (+0.03800), indicating less negative scores in the low-`feature_026`/low-`feature_023` subgroup.
Final synthesis: RPL11 showed adjusted coefficient -0.05587 per SD of `feature_003`, while candidate pairwise dependency interactions did not survive correction.
